examples.title: Sequence Diagram: Withdraw Cash Use Case
examples.description: Pseudocode representation of a sequence diagram for ATM withdrawal
examples.code:
// Objects in the interaction
OBJECT ATMInterface
OBJECT WithdrawalController
OBJECT Account
OBJECT CashDispenser

// Message sequence
ATMInterface -> WithdrawalController: withdrawRequest(amount)
WithdrawalController -> Account: verifyBalance(amount)
Account -> WithdrawalController: balanceResult

ALT balance >= amount THEN
 WithdrawalController -> Account: debit(amount)
 Account -> WithdrawalController: debitResult
 WithdrawalController -> CashDispenser: dispenseCash(amount)
 CashDispenser -> WithdrawalController: dispenseComplete
 WithdrawalController -> ATMInterface: transactionComplete
ELSE
 WithdrawalController -> ATMInterface: insufficientFunds
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows a typical sequence for ATM withdrawal with objects exchanging messages in chronological order. The ALT block shows alternative flows based on balance verification.

examples.title: Activity Diagram: Order Processing
examples.code:
START

ACTIVITY ReceiveOrder
DECISION: ValidateOrder?

IF valid THEN
 ACTIVITY CheckInventory
 DECISION: ItemsAvailable?
 
 IF available THEN
 ACTIVITY ProcessPayment
 DECISION: PaymentSuccessful?
 
 IF success THEN
 ACTIVITY ShipOrder
 ACTIVITY NotifyCustomer
 GOTO END
 ELSE
 GOTO NotifyPaymentFailed
 END
 ELSE
 GOTO NotifyOutOfStock
 END
ELSE
 GOTO NotifyInvalidOrder
END

END
examples.explanation: This represents an activity diagram with decisions, alternate paths, and multiple activities in the order processing use case.

examples.title: State Diagram: Order Lifecycle
examples.description: Pseudocode showing states and transitions for an Order object
examples.explanation: This pseudocode represents a state diagram showing the various states an Order object goes through from creation to delivery, with events causing transitions between states.
